findings.problem: The chapter explains the corridor too many times before the proof scene. Pilar's rooftop reveal already establishes child handoff, badge/lock/transit chaining, and perks-as-retention; the apartment scene then re-runs the same mechanism beat by beat, so the school pickup sequence arrives as confirmation instead of a sharper escalation. That explanatory plateau keeps the chapter below pass-level craft and below shortlist-level award pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reshape the apartment stretch around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:217, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:253, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:273, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:308 so it does not restate the full rooftop lecture from snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:73 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:77. Keep the badge/map/benefits evidence, but make scene two produce either a new operational fact, a new interpersonal wound, or a new cost of manual resistance before the pickup scene at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:582.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if a reader can identify at least one materially new fact or consequence in the revised apartment scene that was not already spoken on the roof, and the pickup sequence adds a fresh pressure beat beyond simply proving Pilar right. FAIL if the apartment scene still mostly restates badge-lock-transit logic and the school scene only confirms it.

findings.problem: The late chapter leans too often on the same correction template: blunt negation, then counter-definition or refinement. Used this many times, the 'No/Not X ... Y' shape stops feeling like pressure and starts reading like a house style tic. The recurrence flattens the prose exactly where the revelation should feel most volatile.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the pressure beats around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:221, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:239, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:470, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:492. Replace several bare correction turns with other sentence shapes: action beats, sensory interruption, longer accumulating syntax, or a line that lands through implication rather than explicit redefinition.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, in the revised passages around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:221 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:470, no more than two pressure beats rely on a bare correction/counter-definition as the whole move and at least three turns land through a different sentence shape or physical action. FAIL if the revised scene still keeps sharpening itself mostly through repeated 'No/Not X ... Y' exchanges.

summary: Dialogue is pass-level: Ben, Nita, Pilar, and Leela keep distinct registers, contraction use stays aligned with the style bible, and I found no continuity or sanitization failure. The chapter still fails overall because the middle over-explains the revelation before the proof scene, and the late prose overuses the same negation/correction sentence shape, which blunts momentum and keeps the chapter below award-level control.
